text:
Today's Digital Daily

Today's Digital Daily

This year marks the 20 anniversary since the British brand first collaborated with the Great Britain team.

Fashion and General Assignments Editor, London

LONDON—Ben Shermanis returning as the official supplier for the BritishOlympicteam for a third time at the Paris 2024 Olympics.

The British brand will dress the team for the opening and closing ceremonies.

This year marks the 20 anniversary sinceBen Shermanfirst collaborated with the Great Britain team at the Athens 2004 Olympics.

“This time, the designs reflect the evolving landscape of British fashion and cultural identity. The ceremonial wear and retail capsule collection for Paris 2024 embodies a fresh perspective characterized by vibrant patterns, intricate detailing and renewed energy,” said Rachel Terrace, chief brand officer at Marquee Brands, the parent company that owns Ben Sherman along with Anti Social Social Club, Martha Stewart and Dakine.Related ArticlesMens FashionBen Sherman Outfits Team GB Winter Olympians, Works With Tom Daley on Knitted Hats, ScarvesEye ScoopsThe Milano Cortina 2026 Olympic and Paralympic Torch Unveiled

The brand has also teamed up with Happy Socks on a capsule collection of socks.You May Also Like

The opening ceremony outfits feature a bomber jacket and an open-collar polo using floral motifs from the four countries of Great Britain.

“Creatively, it’s about building upon the foundation previously established and infusing every stitch and motif with a sense of purpose and pride that resonates with consumers,” said Terrace.

From a business perspective, she hopes teaming up with the BritishOlympicteam will present the brand an opportunity to deepen its connection with its audience and “continue to cultivate meaningful relationships with consumers.”

title: Ben Sherman Returns as Supplier for Team GB at Paris 2024 Olympics
author: Hikmat Mohammed
description: This year marks the 20 anniversary since the British brand Ben Sherman first collaborated with the Great Britain Olympic team.
